goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena — you're reporting high energy this morning with an 87, which is great to hear.

Last night you got 8.8 hours in bed, but your sleep was really fragmented — you were awake nearly 20% of the time, and your efficiency was down at 80%. That's well below your usual, and it tracks with the critically low sleep quality you rated yesterday (33). Your body's recovery signals are telling a stark story: your HRV crashed to 50.1, which is almost half your baseline and still dropping fast. That extreme stress you felt yesterday (95/100) plus this fragmented sleep creates a compounding strain — your nervous system didn't get the deep reset it needed.

Here's what's holding strong: your step count was solid at over 12k yesterday, and your focus score stayed steady at 64 despite the high workload. That's real resilience. But with calories and protein intake declining (1,240 kcal, 65g yesterday) during Ramadan, your body's fuel is low while demand is high. Today is about honoring that gap between how you feel and what your physiology shows — it's a day for deliberate restoration.

Your actions today focus on creating buffers: an earlier evening transition, a breathing reset, and intentional fueling. You've got the discipline from your activity streak — today's about channeling that toward recovery.

assumptions: User is observing Ramadan with Suhoor and Iftar meals
User can adjust evening schedule to support sleep
User has access to alarm/phone for external cues
Nutrition data is incomplete but indicative

evidence_used.historical_patterns: recent sleep onset improvements, persistent nutrition deficits, high workload/stress

soft_constraints: protect evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM
caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep

generated_actions.title: Set a 7:00 PM alarm to close work and begin a 60-minute screen-free transition
generated_actions.rationale: LAST NIGHT's sleep was long but fragmented (efficiency 80.4%, wake ratio 19.45%) and your subjective sleep quality yesterday was critically low (33). Sleep efficiency is declining (fast slope -0.92) and wake ratio is rising (+0.89). The hard constraint 'protect evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM' and today's priority 'sleep_consistency_recovery' make this external cue essential to create a protected buffer for better sleep onset and continuity, aiming to prevent a repeat of high wakefulness.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 7:00 PM alarm help you initiate a screen-free wind-down tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Sleep onset occurs before 8:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1200) AND user reports using the alarm to start a screen-free wind-down routine.

assumptions: You have access to an alarm/phone
You can adjust your evening schedule
generated_actions.description: When your alarm rings at 7:00 PM, stop all work and screens. Dim the lights and spend 60 minutes on gentle, relaxing activities like light tidying, reading a physical book, or gentle stretching to start your wind-down window.

generated_actions.title: During your first afternoon work block, take a 5-minute seated breathing break
generated_actions.rationale: Physiological recovery is critical (HRV 50.1, physio proxy 38) and sharply declining (HRV slope_fast -6.34). YESTERDAY's stress was extreme (95) and rising sharply (+13.3 slope). This micro-break promotes parasympathetic activation to counter the autonomic strain, directly addresses the 'low_recovery' and 'burnout_risk' flags, and fits within a 'tight schedule' and 'high workload'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you take a 5-minute breathing break during your afternoon work block?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing a 5-minute focused breathing break during the afternoon.

assumptions: You can take a short break from work
You can sit comfortably
generated_actions.description: When you start your first focused work block after midday, set a timer for 5 minutes. Sit back, close your eyes, and focus on slow, deep diaphragmatic breathing (4-7-8 pattern) without checking your phone.

generated_actions.title: Add a protein-focused component to your Iftar meal tonight
generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's nutrition showed low intake (1240 kcal, 65g protein) and both are declining sharply (protein slope_fast -3.79, calories slope_fast -89.8). The cross-domain signal 'ramadan_fasting_pattern_with_low_intake' and soft constraint 'follow-up on nutrition confidence gap (0.57)' indicate reinforcing protein today to support energy needs and recovery, addressing the 'nutrition_gap' risk flag.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you intentionally add extra protein to your Iftar meal tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Daily protein intake reaches at least 80g (improving on yesterday's 65g) AND user reports consciously enhancing their evening meal.

assumptions: You are preparing Iftar
You have access to additional protein sources
generated_actions.description: When preparing your Ramadan Iftar, consciously include an additional protein source—like an extra serving of chicken/shish tawook, a hard-boiled egg, or a scoop of Greek yogurt—to boost today's total intake.

generated_actions.title: Finish your Iftar meal by 5:30 PM to extend the pre-sleep fasting window
generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's eating pattern had a compressed window (time_last_meal 300 min) and sleep was fragmented (wake ratio 19.45%). The cross-domain signal 'early_sleep_onset_with_high_wakefulness' and soft constraint 'maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep' suggest that moving Iftar earlier may improve sleep continuity by extending the fasting buffer before bed, directly supporting your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 5:30 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1050), creating a longer buffer before your target early bedtime.

assumptions: You can adjust your Iftar timing slightly
generated_actions.description: Aim to complete your Ramadan Iftar meal by 5:30 PM local time. When you sit down to eat, note the time or set a gentle reminder to wrap up by this earlier cutoff.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Set a 7:00 PM alarm to start your wind-down ritual
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: LAST NIGHT's sleep was critically short (2.83h) and fragmented (efficiency 84.16%, wake ratio 15.84%). Given your severe sleep debt, today's 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority, and the hard constraint to 'protect evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM', this external cue creates a protected buffer to aim for earlier sleep onset and prevent a repeat of the 2.83h duration.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 7:00 PM alarm help you initiate a longer, earlier wind-down tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Sleep onset occurs before 8:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1200) AND user reports using the alarm to start a screen-free wind-down routine.

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When your alarm goes off at 7:00 PM, stop all work, dim the lights, and begin a 60-minute screen-free transition: gentle tidying, reading, or light stretching. Aim to be in bed by 8:00 PM.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: During your first work break, do 3 minutes of seated neck and shoulder rolls
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Physiological recovery collapsed YESTERDAY: HRV plummeted to 47.8 (-12.66 slope_fast) and physio proxy score dropped to 27 (-5.37 slope_fast). This micro-action promotes parasympathetic activation without taxing your strained system, addressing the 'low_recovery' risk flag and the cross-domain signal 'high_activity_amid_recovery_collapse', while respecting the hard constraint of a 'tight schedule'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete 3 minutes of gentle neck and shoulder rolls during a work break today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing the gentle movement and breathing routine during a work break.

assumptions: You can take a short work break
You can sit comfortably
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When you take your first natural break from work, sit back, close your eyes, and slowly roll your neck and shoulders for 3 minutes. Focus on gentle movement and deep breathing, not stretching.

must_avoid: checking your phone during the break

previous_day_generated_actions.title: When you feel 'Annoyed' or tension building, take a 2-minute visual grounding pause
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's stability was strained with emotions 'Annoyed' and 'Alert', and stress was low (34) but declining (-3.73 slope_fast). This implementation intention disrupts potential negative emotional spirals and leverages your reported 'Alert' state for a sensory reset, addressing the 'volatility' risk flag and the cross-domain signal 'subjective_alertness_amid_physiological_depletion'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the visual grounding technique when you felt annoyed or tense today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the 'name 5 things' technique at least once when annoyance or tension arose.

assumptions: You can take brief pauses
You have access to a window or distant view
previous_day_generated_actions.description: If the emotions 'Annoyed' (which you reported yesterday) or physical tension arise, immediately pause, look out a window or at a distant object, and silently name 5 things you can see. Breathe slowly.

must_avoid: continuing the stressful task without pause

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Finish your Iftar meal by 5:30 PM tonight
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's eating window was extremely compressed (last meal at 5:27 AM, time_last_meal 327 min) and sleep was fragmented (wake ratio 15.84%). The cross-domain signal 'compressed_eating_with_sleep_disruption' suggests this pattern may contribute to sleep maintenance issues. Moving Iftar earlier directly addresses this and the soft constraint 'maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep', reinforcing your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 5:30 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1050), creating a longer buffer before your target early bedtime.

assumptions: You can adjust your Iftar timing slightly
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Aim to finish your Ramadan Iftar meal by 5:30 PM local time. When you sit down to eat, note the time or set a gentle reminder to wrap up by this earlier cutoff.

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena. 

You said you're at a 35 this morning — that makes complete sense given what happened overnight. Last night you got only 2.8 hours of sleep, which is critically short and over 2 hours less than your usual. Your body's recovery signals collapsed right alongside it: HRV plummeted to 48, showing severe physiological strain. What's especially telling is that this crash happened while you were exceptionally active yesterday — 14,600 steps and 157 minutes of vigorous movement. That mismatch means your system was pushed far beyond its recovery capacity. 

The data also shows your eating window was compressed, finishing around 5:27 AM, which likely overlapped with that fragmented sleep window. Despite feeling "Alert" yesterday, your body is running on fumes. 

Today is about one thing: damage control and gentle recovery. Your nervous system needs parasympathetic support, not more strain. 

The plan is to protect your evening fiercely and insert micro-breaks to lower the physiological load. 

Given the compounding strain, today's focus is on creating the calmest possible container for your system to begin repairing itself.

evidence_quotes: Today was quite a stressful day but it was productive
I went to pray in thr mosque which was nice and much needed

meal_descriptions.description: Avocado toast with 2 fried eggs, 3 slices of turkey bacon, and a banana and some sliced cucumbers, carrots and bell peppers
Shish tawook and rice and a side salad
